Haiti is highly exposed to hydro-climatic hazards. However
column5: no database exists for the scientific community to characterize these hazards. To fill this gap
column6: hydro-climatic data were compiled to create the first historical database in Haiti. This database
column7: called Simbi
column8:
will contribute to a better knowledge of Haitian catchment hydrology.

Simbi provides hydro-climatic records for 24 Haitian catchments
column9:
available both at the monthly and daily timestep over the 1920-1940 period. Both observed and simulated (using rainfall-runoff models) streamflow series are available for each catchment.

Additionally
column10: the Simbi database contains (i) 156 monthly rainfall series over the 1905-2005 period
column11: (ii) 59 daily rainfall series over the 1920-1940 period
column12: and
column13: (iii) 37 daily rainfall series over the 1948-1966 period
column14:
and (iv) 70 daily streamflow series and (v) 23 monthly air temperature series over the period 1920-1940.

Cette opération de recherche correspond à la réalisation d'une thèse sous le thème « la question de l'accès au logement des ménages à faibles revenus dans l'aménagement de l'espace urbain d'Abidjan : l'expérience du programme des logements sociaux ». Cette thèse a été soutenue publiquement le 25 février 2021 par Mlle TONAN Amenan Sabine à
column2: l'Institut de Géographie Tropicale de l'Université Félix Houphouët Boigny (IGT-UFHB) Abidjan Côte d'Ivoire
column3: sous la direction de Monsieur NASSA Dabié Désiré Axel
column4: Professeur Titulaire de Géographie.    L'étude a été conduite dans le District Autonome d'Abidjan qui a vu la réalisation de vaste programme de logements sociaux suite à l'ambition de l'Etat ivoirien de fournir aux populations à faibles revenus des conditions d'habitation décentes à partir de 2011 et surtout pour corriger ce déséquilibre social urbain né du fonctionnement libre du marché du logement en leur défaveur et de l'abandon par cet Etat à partir de 1980 de toute politique d'habitat social. L'Etat ivoirien a ainsi lancé le Programme Présidentiel des Logements Sociaux (PPLS)
column5: qui exprime la volonté des pouvoirs publics au plus haut niveau à produire 150 000 logements dits sociaux et pour une accession directe à la propriété exclusivement pour la tranche de ménages qualifiés de revenu faible. A ce programme de construction de logements à faibles coût clef en main
column6: l'Etat a joint un projet pilote de Lotissements à Equipement Modéré (LEM)
column7: suivant le modèle des parcelles assainies des années 1970 et 80
column8: avec un plan imposé pour la construction.    L'étude décrit le processus de mise en œuvre de ce programme présidentiel de  logements sociaux et montre que le programme bien qu'orienté vers les revenus faibles
column9: les en exclus au final.  Les thèmes abordés sont : accès au foncier à faible coût
column10: production de logement
column11: aménagement de l'espace urbain. Les principaux questionnements qui ont suscité cette étude sont les suivants : Quel est
column12: dans le contexte néolibéral
column13: le processus engagé par l'État pour mettre à disposition aux promoteurs privés
column14: la géographie des terres nécessaires aux aménagements des logements sociaux ? Comment l'État met-il en œuvre la production des logements sociaux et des infrastructures dans le district d'Abidjan ? Les ménages à revenus faibles trouvent-ils satisfaction à leur besoin d'habiter la ville dans le programme des logements sociaux ? These text and Excel files provide supplementary information for the paper entitled ""Long term evolution of an escarpment in a tableland landscape (Serra Geral de Goiás
column5:
Brazil): insights from in situ-produced cosmogenic nuclides"". The objective of this dataset is to present all the analytical results that support all the conclusions included in this paper.

Among the diversity of geomorphic systems
column6: topographic escarpments are salient relief features besides mountain belts. This study presents a new dataset focused on the Serra Geral Escarpment in Goiás State
column7:
one of the most significant and longest geomorphic features in Central Brazil.
Using in situ-produced cosmogenic nuclides (10Be
column8: 26Al
column9: and 36Cl) concentrations measured in river-borne sediments and bedrock samples
column10: it reveals distinct denudation rates for the main geomorphic compartments of the Serra Geral region. The Western Bahia Plateau exhibits a denudation rate of 3±2 to 9±1 m Myr-1
column11: the Paranã Gap
column12: along the toe of the escarpment
column13: exhibits a rate of 25±1 m Myr-1
column14: and the escarpment shows a rate of 144±40 m Myr-1. The isotopic dataset suggests a complex cosmogenic 26Al/10Be signature
column15: potentially resulting from deep-seated mass wasting in the central section of the escarpment
column16: cave ceiling collapses in the Vão do Paranã
column17: as well as vertical mixing within the thick regolith covering the sandstones capping the Western Bahia Plateau. Preliminary constraints indicate a minimum differential denudation of the relief around 14-24 m Myr-1
column18: an escarpment horizontal retreat rate of 119±21 m Myr-1
column19: and an initiation of pediplanation
column20: resulting to the Patamares at the base of the escarpment during the Upper-Middle Miocene
column21:
approximately 8 to 18 million years ago.
These findings contribute to a better understanding of the geomorphic evolution of topographic escarpment in tablelands and cuesta landscapes. They also raise questions about the impact of karst formations
column23:
and regolith cover on the cosmogenic signal in low denudation settings such as Central Brazil.

Many regions still lack a network of ground weather observations
column4: hampering effective climate monitoring and disaster management. In the Amazon basin

this occurs due to its remoteness and the challenging measurement of rainfall within the forest. Innovative rainfall estimation methods are thus requested to fulfill this gap.

Data distributed here are part of the data acquired during the 2 field campaigns that were carried out in springs 2017 and 2018 in south Tunisia during the WIND-O-V project (https://www6.inrae.fr/anr-windov/) funded by the ANR.

The distributed data document the characteristics of the 1) surface of the eroding field
column6: 2) aerodynamic conditions (wind speed
column7: stability of the surface boundary layer)
column8: 3) saltation flux (intensity and size-distribution)
column9:
and 4) vertical dust flux (intensity and size-distribution) determined by the gradient method. The objective was to explore in depth and understand the links between all these parameters.

Data include:
(1) minimally-dispersed soil size distribution: soil samples were dried at 105°C during 24h before to be slowly sieved on a column of 14 sieves (<50
column22:
>1000 µm). The duration of sieving was 20 minutes and the intensity 40 on a Retsch AS200 vibratory sieve shaker. Each fraction was then weighted on an electronic balance (0.0001 g of precision).
(2) saltation flux: they were measured using five sand traps
column23: the design of which was adapted from the Big Spring Number Eight (BSNE) of Fryrear (1986). Each sand trap had an opening of 5 x 10 cm². They were mounted on a mast with their centers positioned at 5.5
column27: and 87.25 cm above ground level (agl). The mast was equipped with a large wind vane so that the opening of all sand traps always faced the wind. After accounting for the collection efficiency of the sand traps
column28:
the saltation flux was computed by vertical integration of the cumulative masses collected at the 5 heights using the exponential formula proposed by Williams (1964).
(3) aerodynamic parameters (16-min. average): aerodynamic roughness length
column29: and stability parameters were estimated from the wind and temperature profiles using the iterative procedure designed by Frangi and Richard (2000). Wind speed and air temperature vertical profiles were determined using respectively 7 cup anemometers (A100R Vector Instrument®) positioned at 0.22
column36: and 4 thermocouples (type T cupper/Constantan) positioned at 0.48
column39:
and 5.07 m agl on the same mast.
(4) vertical dust flux intensity and size distribution (16-min. average) were determined by the gradient method. Dust concentrations were measured using two identical Optical Particle Counters (OPC) welas® (White Light Spectrometer 2300
column41: each equipped with an omnidirectional total suspended particles (TSP) sampling head (BGI by Mesa Labs
column43: NJ USA) installed at 2.04 m and 4.10 m agl."‡IRD; soils; erosion; saltation; grain size distribution; saltation flux; dust emission event; aerodynamic parameters; vertical dust flux intensity; dataset; Soil sciences; Geography

column3:
Cecile‡"Lien vers les données

This dataset provides 148 Vis-NIR reflectance spectra measured on soil samples collected from the La Peyne catchment (Hérault
column4:
France).

Soil samples

148 soil samples were collected in 2009 on the La Peyne catchment (Herault
column5: France). All of these samples are composed of ﬁve sub-samples that were collected at the topsoil layer at random locations within a 10-m-wide square centered on the geographical position of a sampling plot. For each sample
column6:
approximately 1000 g of soil was extracted. The initial samples were air-dried and sieved with a 2-mm sieve before the laboratory analysis.

Spectral acquisitions

The reﬂectance spectra of the 148 soil samples were measured in the laboratory in the 350–2500 nm wavelength domain with an ASD pro FR portable spectroradiometer. The spectral resolution ranged from 3 nm (at 700 nm) to 10 nm (in the SWIR). The spectral sampling interval varies between 1.4 nm from 350 to 1000 nm and 2 nm from 1000 to 2500 nm. Data are resampled to 1 nm output values. ASD measurements have a SNR better than 1000 at its original spectral resolution. The measurements were made using a black room to control irradiance conditions. A white Spectralon panel (12 × 12 cm labsphere
column7: North Sutton
column8:
USA) provided the absolute reﬂectance factors for laboratory measurements. Thirty individual reﬂectance measurements were acquired for each soil sample.
Further explanations about the methodology can be found in the Peyne2009_ReadMe.txt file.

SOC and texture determination

Granulometry was measured using pipette method and organic carbon was determined after dry combustion minus the inorganic C. Analyses were performed by the Soil Analysis Laboratory of the INRA (Arras
column9: France)
column10:
which is accredited by the French Ministry of Agriculture.

Projects & Fundings

This dataset is part of the project Digisoil-Hymed
column11:
granted by the French National research agency (ANR) (ANR-08-BLAN-0284-01) (2009 - 2012). It has been also supported by IRD and INRAE.
This dataset is also part of the ALAMOD project (2024-2027)
column12:
financed by the PEPR FairCarbon .

This dataset lists the villages involved in the REACT project (Insecticide resistance management in Burkina Faso and Côte d'Ivoire: research on vector control strategies).

Led by three scientific institutions – the Institut de Recherche pour le Développement (IRD
column5: France)
column6: the Institut de Recherche en Science de la Santé (IRSS
column7: Burkina Faso)
column8: and the Institut Pierre Richet (IPR
column9: Côte d'Ivoire) – between 2016 ans 2018
column10: the REACT project aimed to assess whether the addition of complementary vector control strategies to long-lasting insecticidal nets reduces malaria transmission and provides additional protection against malaria in rural areas with a high level of insecticide-resistant vectors. The aforementioned areas were those of Diébougou
column11: southwestern Burkina Faso and Korhogo
column12:
northern Côte d'Ivoire.

Towards this aim
column13: several villages were selected in each area (27 in the Diébougou area and 28 in the Korhogo area) at the beginning of the project according to the following criteria: accessibility during the rainy season
column14: 200 to 500 inhabitants per village
column15: and distance between villages greater than 2 km. Various data (entomological
column18:
etc.) were then collected in these villages during the 2 years-long field phase of the project.

This dataset lists the villages involved in the project and provides a set of useful information for each of them: geographic coordinates
column19: population
column20: type of vector control intervention deployed in the context of the project
column21: name of the health center the village is depending on
column22:
and distance (euclidian and real) to such health center.

This information may be useful for future use of the data that were collected in these villages during the project and subsequently opened (see metadata for the references of the main datasets).

column3:
Sylvain‡"Lien vers les données

As in many tropical deltas globally
column4: annual floods shape the livelihoods of the largely rural population in the Cambodian Mekong delta. Agricultural cycles are keyed to the flood arrival
column5: peak
column6: and recession
column7: and fish populations depend on inundated floodplains for their regeneration. However
column8: as factors like climate change and hydropower infrastructure development are altering the Mekong's hydrology
column9: the inundation dynamics of its deltaic floodplains are shifting as well. Several studies have assessed the general changes of river discharge and flood extent on a basin- or delta-wide scale. Yet the sustainable development of this region is relying on dynamics at more local and specific scales
column10:
which have not been addressed so far.

This dataset includes water level data from nine differential pressure loggers installed in the Kandal region in Cambodia. One of them was installed directly in the Bassac river
column11:
the others in Prek channels - canals that diverge perpendicularly from the river mainstream to establish a connection with low-lying floodplains and acting as vectors for both flooding and drainage.

Data for each logger is in a separate folder
column12: which contains the data
column13: a Readme file with metadata
column14: a diagram of the installation
column15: a map of its location
column16:
and a photograph of the logger in situ.

Accès restreint"‡Étude des déterminants individuels du paludisme asymptomatique dans la zone sanitaire au sud Bénin (2019-2024)‡Cottrell
column3:
Rodeline‡"Lien vers les données

Introduction

Selon l'Organisation Mondiale de la Santé (OMS)
column5: près de la moitié de la population mondiale est exposée au risque du paludisme. Ce fléau constitue un frein au développement des communautés et des pays les plus touchés par la maladie. Après des progrès constants sur la période 2000-2015
column6: les données de la période 2015-2017 mettent en évidence une stagnation sur cette période
column7: voire une régression par endroit (1) ; la plupart des cas (92 %) ont été enregistrés dans la région Afrique de l'OMS. Les 10 pays où le paludisme sévit le plus en Afrique ont rapporté une hausse du nombre de cas en 2017 par rapport à 2016. Au niveau mondial
column8: le nombre de cas pour 1 000 habitants exposés au risque de paludisme a stagné à 59 ces 3 dernières années. Comme dans la plupart des pays de l'Afrique subsaharienne
column9: le paludisme représente la première cause de mortalité et de morbidité au Benin. En 2017
column10: selon l'annuaire des statistiques sanitaires du Ministère de la santé du Bénin
column11: le paludisme constitue la première cause de consultation (44 %) et d'hospitalisation (31 %). Selon les données de l'Enquête Démographique de Santé 2017-2018
column12:
la prévalence de l'infection par le parasite du paludisme chez les enfants de 6-59 mois est de 39 % (microscopie positive à la goutte épaisse).

Parmi les causes pouvant expliquer les derniers résultats préoccupants au niveau mondial évoqués plus haut
column13: les infections asymptomatiques (présence du parasite du paludisme dans l'organisme en l'absence de symptômes) prennent depuis récemment une importance nouvelle. On les suspecte aujourd'hui de jouer un rôle capital dans la perpétuation de la transmission de la maladie (2). En effet
column14: de nombreuses études montrent qu'elles représentent une prévalence élevée dans de nombreuses régions concernées par la maladie
column15: quel que soit le niveau de transmission
column16: et en particulier en Afrique (3). De ce fait
column17: en zone où l'élimination est atteinte
column18: elles constituent une menace de résurgence ; en zone de pré-élimination
column19: elles sont le dernier rempart (invisible) du parasite ; en zone de forte transmission
column20: elles sont probablement l'un des principaux moteurs de l'épidémiologie de la maladie. Néanmoins
column21: elles ont été peu étudiées
column22: et il existe très peu d'études longitudinales visant à étudier leur dynamique et leurs déterminants. Leur épidémiologie reste donc à ce jour mal connue (2)
column23:
et leur compréhension est probablement l'un des éléments clés du succès pour tendre vers l'objectif de l'élimination du paludisme.

Précisément
column24: une meilleure compréhension des infections palustres asymptomatiques et la quantification de la contribution de ce réservoir infectieux à la transmission au vecteur est aujourd'hui indispensable (5) d'un point de vue santé publique. Il est connu que la prévalence à un instant donné des infections asymptomatiques est généralement importante en zone de forte transmission notamment en Afrique (4). La persistance au long cours d'infections asymptomatique dans l'organisme des individus infectés est
column26: beaucoup plus mal cernée. En effet
column27: si de rares publications semblent montrer la persistance de ces infections tout au long de la saison sèche dans un profil épidémiologique sahélien (6)
column28: aucune étude publiée à ce jour ne concerne un profil épidémiologique de paludisme pérenne comme au Bénin (et pays avoisinants). Or la contribution à la transmission et à la diffusion de la maladie dans les populations exposées est bien sûr liée à la persistance des infections chez les individus
column29: et il est donc urgent de se pencher sur cette question. Le but de ce projet de recherche est précisément de décrire la dynamique des infections asymptomatiques au niveau populationnel
column30: sur différentes tranches d'âge et sur une période d'observation de 2 mois
column31:
afin de confirmer l'existence d'un réservoir infectieux au long cours (supérieur à 1-2 mois). Les résultats obtenus pourront permettre de confirmer l'existence d'un réservoir asymptomatique au long cours dans le contexte épidémique du sud du Bénin et d'en faire une description.

Objectifs

Objectif principal

Estimer la proportion de porteurs d'infections asymptomatiques au long cours (excédant 1-2 mois) au sein de 3 tranches d'âge au Sud Bénin.

Objectifs Secondaires

  • Décrire les formes asymptomatiques de paludisme par catégorie d'âge
  • Identifier les facteurs extrinsèques (traitement préventif
column32:
utilisation de MILD) en lien avec un portage asymptomatique du paludisme suivant la catégorie d'âge
  • Estimer le délai de l'évolution d'une forme asymptomatique du paludisme vers une forme symptomatique.

    • Méthode

    Zone et Population d'étude

    Cette étude est réalisée dans la zone sanitaire d'Allada-Zê-Toffo
column33: en communauté. Il s'agit d'une zone située dans le département de l'Atlantique à 70 km de Cotonou. Elle couvre une superficie de 1637 km² (Zè :653 Km²
column34:
Allada :381 Km² et Toffo :492 km²). Elle comporte 24 centres de santé d'arrondissements et 03 Centres de Santé de Communes. Elle dispose depuis le 24 août 2018 d'un Hôpital de référence. Cet hôpital dispose d'un plateau technique de qualité facilitant la prise en charge des urgences. Le paludisme y représente environ 85% des affections enregistrées sur un nombre total de 125 720 cas enregistrées (Annuaire statistique de 2017).

L'étude est réalisée dans un village de la commune de Zê
column35: dont la population est environ égale à 1000 habitants. Les critères de choix des villages sont l'accessibilité
column36:
la disponibilité d'une formation sanitaire aux normes avec présence de personnel qualifié.

L'estimation de la population s'est faite sur la base des données du dernier recensement général de la population. Un ménage est défini comme l'ensemble des personnes habitant dans un même foyer
column37:
partageant un même repas les 6 derniers mois.

Site de l'étude

Cette pré-étude est conduite par le CERPAGE en collaboration avec l'Institut de Recherche pour le Développement (lRD). Le CERPAGE est situé dans la commune de Abomey-Calavi
column38:
dans le département de l'Atlantique.

Design de l'étude

Il s'agit d'une étude prospective qui est conduite au sein de la communauté par des relais communautaires dont les actions sont supervisées par des infirmiers sous la responsabilité d'un médecin épidémiologiste. Le médecin épidémiologiste
column39: coordonnateur de l'étude a assuré la formation des agents sur le terrain
column40: la mise en œuvre de l'étude et le suivi des participants de l'étude suivant le protocole de l'étude et dans le respect des normes éthiques. Le recrutement des participants
column42:
et les visites à domicile se sont faits sous la supervision des infirmiers et du médecin.

column3: Bernard‡Laborde-Balen
column4:
Gabrièle‡"Lien vers les données

In Conakry
column5: Guinea
column6: like many other African capitals
column7: traffic congestion is a major obstacle to transporting blood samples from peripheral health centres to laboratories where tests are centralised. This situation complicates early HIV testing of HIV- exposed children (i.e.
column8: born to HIV-positive mothers)
column9:
even though the World Health Organization recommends treating HIV-infected children before the age of two months to reduce mortality.

The use of drones
column10: which has proved effective in many countries for rapidly transporting healthcare products and reaching isolated areas
column11:
could help to resolve these difficulties and improve early detection.

A pilot study was conducted from 2020–2021 to assess the feasibility
column12:
cost-effectiveness and acceptability of such a strategy. The pilot study had an anthropological component on the acceptability and perception of using drones to optimise the transport of blood samples in Conakry.

Background: Loiasis was long deemed to be a benign condition
column15: but individuals with high Loa loa microfilarial densities (MFD) are at risk of serious adverse events (SAEs) including encephalopathy following ivermectin (IVM) administration. The risk of marked AE or SAE is usually considered when MFD exceeds 8000 microfilariae (mf)/mL or 30
column17: respectively. There are no international guidelines on the treatment of loiasis
column18: resulting in a variety of practices worldwide for the treatment of infected individuals outside endemic areas. Our objective was to determine the probabilities of SAEs after IVM administration at the usual thresholds
column19:
and to refine those thresholds using individual characteristics such as age and sex.

Methods: We used data from two clinical trials conducted in Cameroon where L. loa MFD were determined before IVM administration. The risk of SAE was modeled as a logistic function of age
column20: sex
column21: and MFD transformed as a first-order fractional polynomial. Principal Findings: SAEs probabilities were found to be <104 for MFD<2000 mf/mL
column23: >1% for MFD>20
column26: 000 mf/mL. We showed that specific categories may be at a higher risk of SAE than expected. Specifically
column27: in order not to exceed 1% risk
column29: 000 mf/mL for females in the 31-40 age group; 16
column30: 000 mf/mL for males in the 21-30 age group; 12
column31: 000 mf/mL for males in the 31-40 age group; and 19
column32:
000 mf/mL for males in the 41-50 age group.

Conclusions: Our study suggests that IVM should be used with caution for males or individuals in specific age categories with a high L. loa MFD. For these high risk groups
column33:
lowering the thresholds to 8000 mf/mL should be considered. The increased risk in males requires further investigation to understand the pathophysiological phenomena involved that are crucial to prevent and manage SAEs.

column3:
Cedric‡"Lien vers les données
Background: Loa loa filariasis
column4: a parasitic infection endemic to Central Africa
column5:
is a common cause of medical consultation in this region.

Methods: To evaluate the quality of life (QoL) of individuals living in loiasis-endemic areas
column6: we enrolled 991 subjects (one-third being microfilaremic) from the general population of a rural area in the Republic of Congo. QoL and disability were assessed using WHODAS 2.0 12-items and EQ-5D-5L questionnaires. We collected data on the number of eye worm (Ew) and Calabar swellings episodes experienced throughout their lives
column7:
as well as individual L. loa microfilarial densities and information on infections with soil-transmitted helminthiasis.

Principal findings: Individuals with a history of Ew had a nearly doubled risk of experiencing moderate disability (score >25/100) (adjusted Odds-Ratio=1.77 (95%CI [1.05-2.99]
column9: compared to those without such a history. Those with more than 10 episodes of Ew during their lifetime had a 28% increase in overall disability as measured by WHODAS. No other variable related to loiasis (Calabar swelling frequency
column10: L. loa microfilarial density and positivity to L. loa antibody rapid test) was associated with the various scores. Additionally
column11: infection with Trichuris trichiura was associated to worse anxiety score (adjusted incidence risk ratio=1.22 (95% CI [1.06-1.39]
column12:
p=0.004)). .

Conclusions: The impact of loiasis on daily QoL appears to be primarily due to adult worms rather than microfilarial density. Adult worms would mainly affect daily activities through peripheral symptoms
column13: such as joint-related discomfort (with notable mobility impairment)
column14:
while microfilariae would primarily induce organ dysfunction. Further studies are needed to better understand the respective clinical impacts of adult worms and L. loa microfilariae.

Team projects: https://www.ntd-researchgroup.com/morlo

This dataset contains three runoff and erosion vector maps classified by value ranges: i) a map of runoff coefficient (Krc %) predicted from local surface conditions and runoff data from 535 1- m² plots in Southeast Asia; (ii) a map of mean soil loss (kgm-2) predicted from the same database; and (iii) a map of gully index
column5: which is based on catchment area

column7: a topographic factor. These runoff and erosion maps were produced as part of the ANR FutureHealthSEA project: predictive scenarios of health in Southeast Asia
column8:
linking land use and climate change to infectious diseases.

column9: we assessed soil surface conditions across 100 individual 1-square meter plots
column10: using a robust methodology (Casenave and Valentin
column12: tailored to Southeast Asia (Patin et al.
column13: 2018; Song et al.
column14: 2020). We ensured representation of each land use category with a minimum of four plots
column15: at various topographic positions. Our objective was to determine runoff and soil loss based on these surface conditions
column16:
using a database of 535 1-square meter plots representing a variety of land uses in Southeast Asia (Boithias 2021; available at mtropics.obs-mip.fr/catalogue-m-tropics/).

We conducted multiple linear regressions for the ten land use categories identified in the field to establish statistically significant relationships between the observed surface conditions and annual runoff as well as soil loss (e.g.
column19:
we used Copernicus satellite imagery to identify land use patterns (Mahuzier et al. 2022) within the 287 catchments of the Saen Thong region and generated maps of runoff production (Krc %) and inter-rill erosion (Mean soil loss kg m-2) as predicted by these models.

To address gully erosion on steep slopes (Valentin et al.
column21: we introduced a gully erosion index (Gi = A krc LS)
column22: where ""A"" represented the catchment area in hectares
column23: ""krc"" accounted for the sum of runoff contributions attributed to different land use categories weighted by their respective surface areas within each catchment
column24: and ""LS"" incorporated the topographic factor derived from mean slope steepness and length. We sourced values for ""A"" and ""LS"" from satellite imagery and digital elevation models (DEMs)
column25:
generating the map of this gully erosion index (Gully index).

The bay of Toliara (Madagascar) shelters diverse macroinvertebrate communities in seagrass beds.

This study aims to improve the knowledge of the diversity
column5: abundance
column6:
and socioeconomic role of the seagrass macrobenthos in that area. We studied the mosquito net trawl fishery in seagrass areas in the Bay through the GPS tracking of fishers in 2018-2019. Catches were monitored through monthly landing surveys to characterize spatial temporal patterns in the abundance and diversity of macroinvertebrates in the seagrass beds.

Temporal effects (tide (spring/neap)
column7: months
column8:
and interaction terms) on the mean density and biomass of macroinvertebrate taxa were investigated through a generalized linear model (GLM) using the function « lm » (with a Poisson distribution) of the R software v.3.6.3.

We provide here i) the survey data on seagrass macroinvertebrates collected in 2018-2019 in the Bay of Toliara
column9:
Madagascar using fishers' mosquito net trawls and ii) the R code used for statistical processing.

These text and Excel files provide supplementary information for the paper entitled ""Natural versus anthropogenic erosion in Central Brazil
column5:
a confrontation of time and space scales"" and submitted to Earth's Future for consideration of publication. The objective of this dataset is to present all the analytical results that support all the conclusions included in this paper.

Soil erosion is a global threat to the well-being of billions of people. With the looming global crisis
column6: this systemic problem is likely to get worser
column7: especially in the intertropical zone where many countries are also vulnerable to environmental and socio-economic instabilities. There is still much to learn about the links between natural and human-induced erosion processes
column8: and it remains complex to differentiate them. This study proposes an original combination of techniques
column9: each of them being well recognized in their scientific community but rarely combined to the best of our knowledge. We focused our approach on the region of Brasília
column10: the Cerrado savanna
column11: which is sensitive because of the need of ecosystem conservation and the conflict between preservation of natural spaces and development of human activities. Our study shows how a landscape in a state of equilibrium
column12: where erosion and soil formation rates are in balance
column13: is perturbated by agricultural activities with erosion rates at least 150 times faster
column14:
jeopardizing the sustainability of the soil resource.

Understanding the reproductive biology of a species is an important means of determining the renewal capacity of natural stocks
column4:
especially in the case of heavily exploited species. It is a fundamental element in supporting the implementation of management measures.

Here
column5: we studied the bloody cockle (S. senilis) in the Sine-Saloum
column6:
with the aim of describing its seasonal and spatial reproductive cycle. S. senilis reproduction was studied over an annual cycle at two sites chosen for their contrasting situations along the upstream-downstream gradient.

column7: reproductive investment) of a pool of bloody cockles (Senilia Senilis; n = 200) individuals maintained in-situ in an experimental caging and sampled throughout the year in the Sine Saloum inverse estuary (Senegal)
column8: between March 2021 and March 2022. The reproduction being asynchronous
column9: each stage of maturation for a given individual is represented by its percentage ratio to the total surface area of the gonad. The reproductive investment represents the surface area occupied by the gonad
column10: all stages combined
column11:
over the total surface area of the body section.

column3: Laurent‡Granjon
column4:
Laurent‡"Lien vers les données

Rats of the genus Rattus (especially R. norvegicus the brown rat and R. rattus the black rat) and mice of the genus Mus (especially Mus musculus the domestic mouse) are unanimously recognized as among the “worst” invasive alien species globally. In West Africa
column5: these three species show differential success according to syntheses at the continental level
column6: with the black rat being much more widespread than the domestic mouse
column7: and the brown rat being even less common. Brought to the coasts by sailing ships for centuries
column8: they first settled in coastal colonial trading posts before penetrating inland areas following the main transport routes for goods and people
column9: be they rivers
column10: railways or roads. This scenario has been well documented in Senegal where the invasion routes of the black rat and the domestic mouse
column11: their distribution in the country
column12: and their coexistence with native species within commensal small mammal communities
column13:
are now well known. Data of this kind have also been collected in Niger and Benin by CBGP teams and their local partners

In this frame
column14: the dataset presented here aims at providing information on the commensal small mammal community of the city of Bamako in Mali
column15: a landlocked country of West Africa depending on exchanges with surrounding countries (including Senegal and Benin
column16: among others) for its supplies. It corresponds to the results obtained during 14 trapping campaigns (one week each) that took place between October 2021 and March 2023 in Bamako
column17: capital-city of Mali. The data gathered enable to tackle various questions regarding interactions between native and invasive species
column18: as well as their impacts in various fields
column19:
including as reservoirs of zoonotic diseases.

Elements of the trapping procedures followed here have already been described elsewhere. Briefly
column20: two types of live traps were used: locally made wire-mesh live traps and Sherman folding box traps. Traps were set (in pairs) inside housing or working units including inner yards
column21: between one and three consecutive nights. They were checked and (re)baited once a day with peanut butter spread on a slice of fresh onion. All trap location were precisely geo-referenced and elements of habitat were systematically characterized (room type
column22: presence of food potentially attractive for small mammals
column23: construction materials of floor
column24:
walls and ceiling).

The sampled area corresponds to the district of Bamako
column25: as delimited until 2023. Two quarters per commune in the four communes of the Niger River's left bank and three quarters per commune of the right bank were selected
column26: to account for the larger size and population of the right bank communes. Quarters were selected as to be representative of intra-communal diversity in terms of urbanization and socio-economic characteristics
column27: without being too close from each other in order to ensure good spatial coverage of the city. In the quarters that were large enough (i.e. most of them)
column29: separated by at least a few hundred metres from each other. The sampled housing units were chosen at random
column30:
with a spacing between them of at least 50m.

The whole represents a total effort of 8092 trapnights
column31: that yielded 1438 captures of small mammals. More than 83% of them were rodents
column33: Rattus rattus and Mus musculus) and native (Mastomys natalensis
column34:
Cricetomys gambianus) species. The remaining captures were of the shrew Crocidura olivieri.

En Côte d'Ivoire
column4: la prévalence du VIH chez les TS est d'environ 5% soit 2
column5: 5 fois plus qu'en population générale. Bien que le préservatif soit généralement bien utilisé
column6: il existe des exceptions (non utilisation avec un partenaire régulier
column7: un client violent ou contre une forte somme d'argent). Aussi
column8: les TS ont des besoins en santé sexuelle et reproductive (SSR) autres que le VIH
column9: tels que la prévention et la prise en charge des IST et de l'hépatite B
column10: ou encore la gestion de la contraception pour éviter les grossesses non désirées. Dans ce contexte
column11: la prophylaxie préexposition (PrEP) peut être un outil privilégié de prévention. Mais
column12:
il est essentiel d'évaluer dans quelles conditions offrir la PrEP.

L'objectif général de l'étude était de développer
column13: documenter et analyser une offre de soins communautaire combinant dépistage
column14: prévention combinée dont PrEP
column15: traitement immédiat du VIH
column16:
prise en charge de l'hépatite B et santé sexuelle et reproductive (SSR).

Cette offre de santé était disponible à la fois dans des cliniques mobiles organisées pour un suivi trimestriel (10 sites d'interventions
column17:
chaque site étant visité toutes les deux semaines) et dans la clinique fixe de l'ONG communautaire Aprosam.

Ce jeu de données présente les métadonnées quantitatives de la cohorte ANRS 12381 PRINCESSE et fournit
column19: (1) le protocole complet de l'étude incluant les notices d'information et les formulaires de consentement
column20:
(2) la liste des questionnaires et des fiches cliniques de l'étude ; et (3) la liste et le dictionnaire des variables des tables de données analytiques du projet.

La cohorte ANRS 12381 PRINCESSE est une cohorte interventionnelle mono-bras
column21: mise en oeuvre de novembre 2019 à juin 2023 dans la région de San Pedro et ses alentours en Côte d'Ivoire
column22: auprès d'un public de femmes travailleuses du sexe (TS)
column23: et incluant une d'une offre de soins globale en santé sexuelle et reproductive
column24: combinant une offre de prophylaxie préexposition du VIH (PrEP) orale (pour les TS VIH-) et une prise en charge précoce des femmes VIH+
column25: la prise en compte de l'infection par le VHB (dépistage
column27: le dépistage et le traitement des IST et de leurs conséquences
column29: un dépistage trimestriel des grossesses
column30:
un conseil en gestion des menstrues et le repérage des situations de dépendance.

Le protocole détaillé est disponible sur : https://hal.science/ANRS_12381_PRINCESSE/hal-04127364.
Une version courte en anglais a été publié et est disponible à https://hal.science/ANRS_12381_PRINCESSE/hal-03467822.
L'ensemble des publications liées au projet sont accessibles à https://hal.science/ANRS_12381_PRINCESSE.

column3: Lucie‡Penicaud
column4:
Juliette‡"Lien vers les données

These data were collected in the frame of Juliette Penicaud's PhD at LEGOS (Toulouse
column5: France)
column6: whose subject is the model-based study of dynamics and hydrosedimentary processes in the riverine estuary and in the plume of the Red River
column7: East Sea of Vietnam. The general objective of the thesis is to develop and deepen the expertise in terms of modelling and observation of the estuary and river plumes in the Haiphong area in order to reproduce their dynamics and to analyse the hydrosedimentary processes on the basis of numerical models
column8: benefiting for their calibration and validation from all archive or future data
column9: in situ or satellite. Supervision of the PhD: Sylvain Ouillon & Marine Herrmann (IRD
column10: LEGOS)
column11: with the strong involvement of Vu Duy Vinh (IMER
column12: VAST
column13:
Haiphong).

The Red River brings to the Gulf of Tonkin considerable quantities of fresh water (3700 m3/s) and suspended matter (45 Mt/year). This region
column14: whose coasts are very densely populated (approx. 30M inhabitants in the Red River delta)
column15: is subject to variability factors of different origins (atmospheric
column19: from extreme events (cyclones) to climate change (sea level rise is particularly sensitive in the delta region).  In estuaries
column20:
less dense freshwater flows over marine salt water with great variability in space and time.

As a continuation of this work
column21: new projects are currently starting on the analysis of river plumes in the Haiphong area (Cam Bach Dang and Van Uc rivers): <UL> <LI>Plume dispersion analysis based on monitoring of Lagrangian drifters (Alexei Sentchev
column23: ADCP) <LI>Concomitant measurements of suspended particles (turbidity
column25: grain size distribution) <LI>In-situ characterization of the spatial and temporal variability of suspended matter (in-situ measurement of grain size distribution
column27:
long-shore and cross-shore variability)

The Van Uc River is a typical distributary of the Red River
column28: with rice crops all along the banks that require water and construction of dikes for flooding protection
column29: and with constant river bed dredging for navigation purposes. The Van Uc estuary is located next to Haiphong city (third largest city of Vietnam). It varies in width from ~350 to ~450 m. The Van Uc River is ranked at the third position of the Red River system in terms of both water (14.5% of the total water discharge from the Red-Thai Binh system to the Gulf of Tonkin) and sediment discharge (14.4% of the total sediment flux of the Red-Thai Binh River to the Red River coastal area. Van Uc river is influenced by a strong seasonal river signal associated with the monsoon. Indeed
column30:
the wet summer season (June to September) accounts for 85-95% of the total annual rainfall in the region.

In-situ measurement campaigns were carried out to study estuarine processes. Field surveys have been performed in the Van uc estuary and in the close coastal plume at different stages of the wet season (June
column33:
always in spring tides.

Two sampling strategies have been employed. The first strategy consisted in longitudinal transects of around 15 to 20 km in the estuary performed at various tide levels. Stations were spaced around 2 km and efforts were made to bring closer together the stations where important turbidity was measured and when we discovered rapid change in salinity. The transects were made in around 3h and performed in both senses (from upstream to downstream and opposite). Transect stations extend from 13 km upstream to 9 km downstream the mouth.

The second measurement strategy consisted in a fixed station near the mouth of the river of 24h in October
column34:
in order to follow the evolution of the same part of the estuary over the full tidal cycle.

During these transects and fixed stations we measured continuously the current velocity Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler (ADCP). At each station
column37: density and turbidity were measured using a profiling CTD Compact. Another CTD
column39: was used to perform instant profiles and sharpen the transects strategy
column40:
i.e. adapting better the evolution in salinity.

column3:
Sassan S‡"Lien vers les données
BACKGROUND
For malaria mosquitoes
column4: the ability to detect pyrethroid insecticides before coming into contact with an impregnated surface can be highly advantageous in terms of fitness. Such detection may lead to the development of behavioral modifications that help them to avoid commonly used vector control tools. A previous study has shown that malaria mosquitoes can detect different pyrethroid molecules without coming into contact with them
column5: regardless of their physiological resistance status. However
column6: the mechanisms underlying this detection are not yet fully understood. This study aimed to identify which chemosensory appendages in Anopheles gambiae are involved in the non-contact detection of permethrin
column7:
a widely used pyrethroid in malaria control.

RESULTS
Behavioral responses to commercial permethrin headspace were recorded in female An. gambiae
column8: in which specific sensory appendages were either removed or coated with resin to impair their chemosensory function. Additionally
column9: electrophysiological recordings were performed on different sensory appendages: antennae
column10: palpi and tarsi
column11: to characterize their electrophysiological activity after permethrin stimulation. The behavioral assays revealed that tarsi were primarily responsible for mediating mosquito takeoff responses after permethrin headspace delivery. This finding was supported by significant electrophysiological tarsal responses to the insecticide. In contrast
column12: removal of the antennae did not alter behavioral responses
column13:
although electroantennogram recordings indicated neural activity in response to the permethrin headspace. The palps showed neither behavioral nor electrophysiological responses.

CONCLUSION
These findings indicate that An. gambiae detects commercial permethrin in vapor form using two distinct sensory appendages: the tarsi and the antennae
column14: but with varying behavioral output. The results suggest that the non-contact detection of the insecticide may be mediated by chemosensory signaling pathways. Nonetheless
column15: further research is required to identify the specific sensory mechanisms involved in the detection of pyrethroids in malaria mosquitoes
column16:
and their contribution to the evolution of behavioral adaptations against pyrethroid-based vector control tools.

column3: Lucie‡Piton
column4:
Violaine‡"Lien vers les données

These data were collected in the frame of Violaine Piton's PhD at LEGOS (Toulouse
column5: France)
column6: whose subject is to study the dynamics and sediment transport along the estuary-coastal area continuum. The evolution of the Red River delta in terms of siltation
column7: erosion and accretion of its estuaries and coastlines constitute a major concern
column8: as the delta is the most populated region of Vietnam
column9: and is the second-most important rice-producing region of the country. The Red River was ranked as the ninth river in the world in terms of sediment discharge but the construction of two dams across the river in the 1980s caused a reduction of sediment discharge from 145-160 Mt y-1 in the 1970s (Milliman and Meade
column10: 1983) to 40 Mt y-1 nowadays (Le et al.
column11: 2007; Vinh et al. 2014; Wei et al.
column12:
2019).

In order to explore the seasonal and tidal changes in flow and suspended matter dynamics in the tropical and macrotidal Van Uc river estuary (North Vietnam) and in order to better understand the sediment delivery to the ocean and the processes behind the estuarine siltation
column13:
four in-situ measurement campaigns were carried out in 2017.

The campaigns took place during the contrasting high flow and low flow seasons
column14: for each during neap and spring tides. This dataset corresponds to the wet season
column15: spring tide. Water and suspended matter fluxes
column17: turbidity and particle size distribution (PSD) were measured for 24h at three cross sections along the estuary. These three stations (ST1
column18: ST2 and ST3) are spaced by 10km. ST1 is located near to the river mouth while ST3 is the further upstream. Each station was sampled for 24 hours with cross-river transects measurements every two hours to cover a full diurnal tidal cycle
column19:
starting at ST1 and finishing at ST3.

Each series of measurements consisted of four different steps. Firstly
column20: cross river velocity profiles were measured will a hull-mounted
column21: downward-looking
column22: 1200 kHz Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler (ADCP RDI Workhorse in bottom tracking mode). Secondly
column23: vertical profiles of conductivity and temperature were measured for each transect
column24: in the deep channel at the middle of the river
column29: now released by JFE Advantech Co.
column32: as Rinko-Profiler) at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. Thirdly and parallel to CTD measurements
column33: depth profiles of floc size distribution and concentration were measured using an in-situ laser scattering and transmissometry instrument with a 90% path reduction module (LISST-100X
column39: in order to study the relationship between turbidity and suspended particulate matter concentration
column40:
water samples (~1 L) near subsurface (~1.5 m depth) were collected every four hours with a Niskin bottle. Turbidity of these samples were measured onboard using a Hach 2100Q Turbidimeter.

column3: provided that appropriate credit is given. License text: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/‡Territorial nitrogen budget and metabolic typologies of European agri-food systems
column5: Ludovic‡Harter
column6:
Ludovic‡"Lien vers les données
This dataset was developed to implement the GRAFS (Generalized Representation of Agri-Food Systems) approach and provides harmonized nitrogen (N) flows and budgets across agri-food systems for 120 territories across Europe
column7: mainly at the subnational level
column8: covering the period 1990-2019. It builds on previous European-scale studies by offering consistent and spatially explicit estimates of N inputs
column9: outputs
column10: and internal flows within arable land
column11: permanent cropland
column12: and permanent grassland systems. The dataset includes annual estimates of N content in harvested crops and the areas they cover
column13: categorized by crop type
column14: as well as N inputs from synthetic fertilizers
column15: manure application
column18: and ammonia volatilization. It also provides estimates of feed and food imports
column19: animal production
column20: and the allocation of N from cropland and grassland to local human and livestock consumption
column21: as well as exports beyond the territory. All data are based on publicly available sources
column23: and have been integrated using standardized correction and gap-filling procedures to ensure consistency and temporal continuity. Each data point includes a confidence flag indicating whether it is raw
column26:
with full documentation of the methods used.

The harmonized dataset covers a total of 120 territories over a 30-year period. It is organized as follows: (i) for each of the 16 aggregated crop categories
column27: we report the following three variables: total harvested area (Mha)
column29: and N yield (kg N/ha). (ii) We include total areas (in Mha) for the following major land use types: permanent grassland
column32: arable land and total cropland. (iii) N deposition rates to cropland are reported in kg N/ha. (iv) Symbiotic N fixation inputs are reported for pulses
column35: and soybeans (all in Gg N). (v) Total synthetic fertilizer application to cropland is disaggregated into applications to arable land
column36: permanent grassland and permanent crops (Gg N). (vi) Total N excretion is estimated per animal category (8 categories; Gg N). (vii) We report the N content (Gg N) for animal products (milk
column38: disaggregated by species and by edible and non-edible parts)
column41: and imported feed. (viii) We include estimates of total excretion
column44: grazing excretion on permanent and temporary grassland
column45: and manure application to different land use types (permanent grassland
column47: permanent crops and other arable land)
column48:
all expressed in Gg N.

The resulting dataset is provided in a tidy csv format to facilitate efficient access to and processing of the data. Each file includes a ""confidence"" column documenting the treatment applied to each data point
column49: referencing the methods detailed in the supplementary material. The ""unprocessed"" flag indicates that the value corresponds to raw data extracted directly from the sources (mainly Eurostat) at the same spatial resolution. However
column50: this flag does not necessarily imply greater reliability
column51: given that numerous inconsistencies were identified in Eurostat datasets. The ""filled"" flag denotes that missing values were estimated using national-level data distributed across subnational territories. This flag is always accompanied by a letter referring to the specific filling procedure described in the supplementary material. The ""interpolated"" flag indicates that the value was obtained through linear interpolation or extrapolation. Finally
column52: the ""corrected"" flag refers to values that were adjusted because the original source was deemed unreliable. column3:
Serge‡"Lien vers les données

The Millennium Coral Reef Mapping Project provides thematic maps of coral reefs worldwide at geomorphological scale. Maps were created by photo-interpretation of Landsat 7 and Landsat 8 satellite images.

Maps are provided as standard Shapefiles usable in GIS software. The geomorphological classification scheme is hierarchical and includes 5 levels. The GIS products include for each polygon a number of attributes. The 5 level geomorphological attributes are provided (numerical codes or text). The Level 1 corresponds to the differentiation between oceanic and continental reefs. Then from Levels 2 to 5
column4: the higher the level
column5: the more detailed the thematic classification is. Other binary attributes specify for each polygon if it belongs to terrestrial area (LAND attribute)
column6: and sedimentary or hard-bottom reef areas (REEF attribute).  Examples and more details on the attributes are provided in the references cited. The products distributed here were created by IRD
column7:
in their last version.

Finalized versions of the data files and related documentations will be openly accessible in April 2023."‡Consumption of traditional cowpea-based dishes in urban and rural areas of South Benin‡Mouquet-Rivier
column3:
Lorène‡"Lien vers les données

Regular consumption of legumes is recommended worldwide for its environmental and health benefits. Cowpea
column4: the most-frequently consumed pulse in West African countries
column5: is rich in nutrients and health-promoting bioactive compounds. In southern Benin
column6:
a wide variety of cowpea-based dishes are observed.

To quantify the consumption of cowpea-based dishes by the Beninese population
column7: a one-week retrospective Food Frequency Questionnaire and a food atlas were used.  Participants were 1217 adults (19-65 years) from three urban or rural areas in southern Benin : 641 adults in Cotonou (surveyed in 2017)
column8: and 576 in Adjohoun and Allada (288 respondents per rural area
column9:
surveyed in 2019).
Data were collected on digital tablets using SurveyCTO™. The FFQ included two parts. In the first part
column10: data on the household head's socio-professional characteristics
column11: household income and expenses
column12: household belongings
column13: and housing characteristics were collected to characterize the household socio-economic index. In the second part
column14: data on the consumption of cowpea-based dishes and the estimation of the quantities consumed over one week were recorded. During the FFQ-based interviews
column15: general questions about the consumption of various legume species were first asked. This was followed by questions on the cowpea-based dish types consumed by the respondent during the previous week (7-day recall). For each cowpea-based dish
column16: we recorded how many times it was consumed
column18:
and the quantity usually consumed (estimated using the food atlas).

The research protocols describing all procedures that implicated human participants in Cotonou and in the rural areas were submitted to the Benin National Committee of Ethics for Health Re-search
column19:
and approved. The clearance numbers are N° 29 (2017) and N°14 (2019) for the survey in Cotonou and in the rural areas respectively.
Informed Consent Statement: All respondents gave their signed consent to participate before the start of each interview. The respondents' anonymity was respected
column20:
as stated in the consent form.

column3: Adeline‡Barnaud
column4:
Adeline‡"Lien vers les données

Marginal land is land that is of little agricultural value because crops produced there would be worth less than any rent paid for access to the area. Helping to develop such zones in Africa is a key goal for the EU-funded EWA-BELT project. The aim is to promote the sustainable intensification of agricultural production in organic
column5:
agroforestry and mixed crop and livestock farming systems in 38 study areas of six countries belonging to East and West Africa. The project will enhance current scientific knowledge on the adaptation of new and improved traditional crops in different agroecosystems and the impacts of traditional agricultural practices on soil health.

This initiation to genomic data analysis with R was designed within the EWA-BELT H2020 EU project (grant agreement ID 862848).

The training module introduced to a variety of population genomics analyses for genotyping by sequencing (GBS) datasets. It included an introduction to single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) calling and filtering options
column6: experimental design and various applications
column7: population genomics statistics (genetic diversity and structure
column8:
Fst-based analyses) and genome-wide association studies (GWAS).

The majority of the course consisted of practical R sessions to give hands-on experience of these analyses.

Main topic and contents treated during the courses :
Session 1. Passport data and Bioinformatics analysis
Session 2. VCF filtering
Session 3. Genetic diversity and structure
Session 4. Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)

Each session includes a short introduction outlining the core concepts (PDF)

L'intérêt de la station Bamako pompage est de doubler l'échelle de Bamako Ville dont les lectures de basses eaux étaient imprécises.
Time series of water levels for 2 hydrometric stations in Bamako on the Niger river in Mali. The computerized water level data come from digitized documents available in the IRD HORIZON bibliographic database. The website of the “Direction Nationale de l'Hydraulique du Mali” provides water level readings in the form of a hydrological bulletin: www.dnhmali.org
Some corrections have been made to the average daily levels by checking the limnigrams. Some completions were made for days not observed or forgotten during data entry. The interest of the “Bamako pompage” station is to be a back-up of the Bamako City station whose low water readings were imprecise."‡IRD; hauteur d'eau; water level; Bamako; Niger; Mali; Hydrology; INRAE‡Observational data‡"Sierem_Biblio_HE_Bamako.pdf; Sierem_DescriptionFichierHauteurEeauHydromet.pdf; Sierem_ExpNigerBamakoPompageHE.tab; Sierem_ExpNigerBamakoVilleHE.tab; Sierem_Graph_HE_NigerBamakoetBamakoPompage.PNG; Sierem_Graph_HE_Niger_Bamako.PNG; Sierem_Graph_HE_Niger_BamakoPompage.PNG; SIEREM_LOGO_HE_BAMAKO.jpg"‡‡‡French‡-8.129058934282511‡-7.810455418657512‡‡‡Start Date: 1941-01-01 ; End Date: 1979-12-31

Contents of the ASI

The Annuaire statistique de l'Indochine is a general yearbook that does not focus solely on meteorological and climatic data. It was published between 1927 and 1948. As it's a continuation of Henri Brenier's Atlas statistique de l'Indochine
column4: published in 1914
column5: the last year of which was 1912
column6: the ASI tried to fill the gap by publishing statistics from 1913 onwards and sometimes by starting earlier
column7:
as in the case of meteorology.

The Yearbook is generally organized into 15 chapters as follows:

  1. Climatology
  2. Territory and people
  3. Education
  4. Health
  5. Justice and prisons
  6. Agriculture
column8:
livestock and forestry
  • Industry
  • Means of transport and communication
  • Money
column9:
banking
  • Trade
  • Property and income
  • Consumption
  • Military and administrative personnel
  • Public finance
  • Territory of Kouang Tchéou Wan

Details on the content of each dataset:
  • HoopCardCorrelation : counts of yellow crazy ants using either the hoop or the card methods at the same place to compare the two methods
  • HoopRepeatability: repetition of yellow crazy ant counts at the same place with the hoop method to measure the repeatability of the hoop count method
  • OnetahiTemperatureEffect: repeated counts of yellow crazy ants at the same sites at different moments of the day and temperature to assess the effects of temperature and time of the day on the number of ants counted
  • PlotHabitatYCACrabsBRNO: variables describing the content of the plots monitored to assess yellow crazy ant relative abundance

Here
column8: we introduce GraTools (https://forge.ird.fr/diade/gratools)
column9:
a fast and user-friendly command-line tool for manipulating PVGs using the original GFA file.
After a one-time graph import
column10: GraTools enables rapid subgraph extraction
column11: fasta sequence retrieval
column12: and comprehensive analyses
column13: including core/dispensable genome ratio calculation or group-specific segment identification. The import step results in conversion in standard data formats (BAM/BED)
column14: enabling the reuse of well-optimized existing tools
column15: allowing an efficient storage and the querying of the PVGs large complex data structures. Scalability is ensured by a modular architecture supporting parallel processing and asynchronous I/O operations. GraTools supports coordinates defined on both the primary reference as well as from alternative genomes within the graph without re-importing
column16:
and its outputs can be easily visualized or manipulated using external tools.
Using an Asian rice pangenome graph (13 accessions)
column17: we demonstrate its ability to easily extract subgraphs
column19: and identify subspecies-specific segments. An intuitive command-line interface
column20: a real-time execution feedback and a detailed logging system make this tool suitable for a wide range of applications
column21: from population genetics to breeding and genomic medicine

column3:
Ganna‡"Lien vers les données

This dataset contains additional supporting information and source code related to the discovery of Xanthomonas-induced small RNAs (xisRNAs) as described in the Related Publication.

Deep small RNA (sRNA) sequencing analysis discovered a class of sRNA in rice (Oryza sativa) specifically associated with foliar diseases caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pathovars. A set of 163 xisRNAs loci were distinctively upregulated in response to various bacterial strains at an early stage of infection. Most xisRNA loci overlap with annotated cis-transcripts sequences often encoding protein kinase domain proteins including RLKs and RLCKs. A number of the corresponding rice cis-genes have documented functions in immune signaling and about one third of the xisRNA loci coincide with the coding sequence of a conserved kinase motif. xisRNAs exhibit features of small interfering RNAs and their biosynthesis depend on canonical components OsDCL1 and OsHEN1. Notably xisRNAs production was dependent on the X. oryzae Type III secretion system
column4: a major bacterial virulence factor for host colonization. xisRNA induction possibly mediates post-transcriptional gene silencing but they do not appear to widely suppress cis-genes expression on the basis of mRNA-seq data. Overall
column5:
our results identify an unrecognized group of unusual sRNAs with a potential role in plant-microbe interactions.

The main entry point for this dataset is the ""PHIM_ xisRNA_MS_dataverse_code.html"" file that records the code and the output of most secondary analysis computing tasks performed to generate the material relevant to the manuscript on xisRNAs referenced in the Related Publication metadata. Other Files include selected data files that have been used or produced in the course of the analysis workflow.

The Millennium Coral Reef Mapping Project provides thematic maps of coral reefs worldwide at geomorphological scale. Maps were created by photo-interpretation of Landsat 7 and Landsat 8 satellite images.

Maps are provided as standard Shapefiles usable in GIS software. The geomorphological classification scheme is hierarchical and includes 5 levels. The GIS products include for each polygon a number of attributes. The 5 level geomorphological attributes are provided (numerical codes or text). The Level 1 corresponds to the differentiation between oceanic and continental reefs. Then from Levels 2 to 5

column3:
Veerle‡"Lien vers les données
In the last decade
column4: the prevalence of Trypanosoma brucei gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (HAT) has fallen and HAT has been targeted for elimination.  Clinical trials on safe and efficacious drugs for HAT
column5: applicable in an elimination context
column6:
would be accelerated by availability of an early test of cure. The objective of the DiTECT-HAT-WP4 study was to validate the diagnostic performance of cerebrospinal fluid neopterin quantification and of blood and cerebrospinal fluid trypanosomal spliced leader RNA detection for assessing treatment outcome.

The DiTECT-HAT-WP4 study was embedded into a therapeutic phase II/III study
column7: DNDi-OXA-02-HAT
column8: testing a new oral single dose drug against HAT
column9: acoziborole.  After acoziborole treatment
column10: patients had post-treatment examinations
column11: including blood and cerebrospinal fluid examination at day 11
column12: and during follow-up at month 6
column13: month 12 and month 18.  Combination of DiTECT-HAT-WP4 with the DNDi-OXA-02-HAT study in  5 study sites in RD Congo
column14: allowed evaluation of new treatment outcome assessment markers during follow-up in 97 confirmed HAT patients
column15: without the need for additional lumbar or venipunctures.  The volumes of venous blood and cerebrospinal fluid taken was increased by 2.5 mls for the DiTECT-HAT-WP4 study.  Reverse transcriptase real time PCR for spliced leader RNA detection in blood and cerebrospinal fluid and neopterin detection were carried out in the reference laboratory in Kinshasa (index tests).  For evaluation of diagnostic performance of the index tests
column16: the reference standard consisted of classification of treatment outcome according to international standards applied for the DNDi-OXA-02-HAT trial but excluding for DiTECT-HAT-WP4 patients lost to follow-up (including those who died for other reasons than HAT).  Sensitivity and specificity of the different index tests for treatment outcome assessment was determined at each follow-up time point.  Trypanosomal spliced leader RNA detection in blood may allow post-treatment follow-up without the need for lumbar punctures.  Improved treatment outcome assessment will not only facilitate follow-up by avoiding the feared lumbar puncture but also speed up the development and implementation of new drugs.  In addition
column17:
it will also improve management of patients in routine.

The deposited files contain the DiTECT-HAT-WP4 study protocol and the informed consent form. After publication
column18: DiTECT-HAT-WP4 data will be made available to qualified researchers
column19: upon request after signing a confidentiallity agreement. Data requests may be sent to the coordinating investigator (for coordinates see study protocol)."‡IRD; Trypanosomiasis

The dataset contains classification results of radar altimetry echoes performed with the unsupervised K-means method (netCDF4 files) with each satellite missions (ENV for ENVISAT and SRL for SARAL) based on echo backscatter and normalized water height (sh) parameters. For each area
column7: four classes were created to best represent the different hydrological regimes in the Amazon without over-interpreting radar echoes. The four classes are rated from 0 to 3. Class 0 is very often located on open water (lakes or rivers); on the contrary
column8: class 3 is mainly located in non-flooded areas (not used here). Classes 1 and 2 correspond to intermediate hydrological environment such as permanently or seasonally flooded and seasonally or infrequently flooded
column9:
respectively.

For each cluster of echoes classified in the same class along an altimetric track
column10: a station is created containing the time series of local water level variation (.txt files). Ce(tte) œuvre est mise à disposition selon les termes de la Licence Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International."‡Supplementary data for: Transpiration efficiency variations in the pearl millet reference collection PMiGAP‡Vigouroux
column3:
Yves‡"Lien vers les données

Transpiration efficiency (TE)
column4: the biomass produced per unit of water transpired
column5: is a key trait for crop performance under limited water. As water becomes scarce
column6:
increasing TE would contribute to increase crop drought tolerance.

This study is a first step to explore pearl millet genotypic variability for TE on a large and representative diversity panel. We analyzed TE on 537 pearl millet genotypes
column7: including inbred lines
column8: test-cross hybrids
column9: and hybrids bred for different agroecological zones. Three lysimeter trials were conducted in 2012
column11: to assess TE both under well-watered and terminal-water stress conditions. We recorded grain yield to assess its relationship with TE. Up to two-fold variation for TE was observed over the accessions used. Mean TE varied between inbred and testcross hybrids
column12: across years and was slightly higher under water stress. TE also differed among hybrids developed for three agroecological zones
column13: being higher in hybrids bred for the wetter zone
column14:
underlining the importance of selecting germplasm according to the target area.

Environmental conditions triggered large Genotype x Environment (GxE) interactions
column15: although TE showed some high heritability. Transpiration efficiency was the second contributor to grain yield after harvest index
column16:
highlighting the importance of integrating it into pearl millet breeding programs. Future research on TE in pearl millet should focus (i) on investigating the causes of its plasticity i.e. the GxE interaction (ii) on studying its genetic basis and its association with other important physiological traits.

This dataset contains raw data of experiment on the transpiration efficiency variations in the pearl millet reference collection PMiGAP.

Dans le cadre du projet STREESCO (STREngthening Epidemiological Surveillance in Benin and Burkina Faso for an effective response to COVID-19)
column7: la collecte systématique de données sur les résultats sanitaires comprend des méthodes de surveillance basées sur les événements
column8:
les patients hospitalisés et les laboratoires. Elle a trois objectifs spécifiques :

  1. Mettre en place au Bénin dans des sites stratégiques pour le pays (à la frontière du cordon sanitaire et en dehors du cordon sanitaire) une surveillance épidémiologique active des cas suspects selon le protocole de l'OMS
column9:
en appui à la stratégie nationale de réponse à la CoVID-19.
  • Renforcer cette stratégie nationale en développant un système de surveillance épidémiologique clinique dans les zones reculées du Bénin (approche des centres de santé) et du Burkina Faso (approche des enquêtes de population) Ces actions permettront de suivre l'évolution de l'épidémie et d'alerter en temps réel les autorités sanitaires
column10: en détectant et en comptant les cas
column11:
en identifiant les groupes (liens entre les cas et les contacts) et en surveillant l'évolution de l'épidémie (seuils de réaction).
  • Mieux comprendre la dynamique de l'épidémie et ses paramètres en Afrique grâce à une analyse biostatistique et géo-épidémiologique moderne des données collectées dans le cadre de ce projet. Cette veille sanitaire mise en place dans les établissements de santé permettra de capter
column12:
filtrer et analyser les signaux relatifs à l'émergence épidémique des infections respiratoires virales. Cette dernière action devrait permettre la détection d'une éventuelle nouvelle vague de CoVID-19 ou de sa résurgence.

Ce jeu de données décrit (répertorie) les données du projet
column13:
accompagné d'une partie des documentations associées.

column3: Lucie‡Piton
column4:
Violaine‡"Lien vers les données

These data were collected in the frame of Violaine Piton's PhD at LEGOS (Toulouse
column5: France)
column6: whose subject is to study the dynamics and sediment transport along the estuary-coastal area continuum. The evolution of the Red River delta in terms of siltation
column7: erosion and accretion of its estuaries and coastlines constitute a major concern
column8: as the delta is the most populated region of Vietnam
column9: and is the second-most important rice-producing region of the country. The Red River was ranked as the ninth river in the world in terms of sediment discharge but the construction of two dams across the river in the 1980s caused a reduction of sediment discharge from 145-160 Mt y-1 in the 1970s (Milliman and Meade
column10: 1983) to 40 Mt y-1 nowadays (Le et al.
column11: 2007; Vinh et al. 2014; Wei et al.
column12:
2019).

In order to explore the seasonal and tidal changes in flow and suspended matter dynamics in the tropical and macrotidal Van Uc river estuary (North Vietnam) and in order to better understand the sediment delivery to the ocean and the processes behind the estuarine siltation
column13:
four in-situ measurement campaigns were carried out in 2017.

The campaigns took place during the contrasting high flow and low flow seasons
column14: for each during neap and spring tides. This dataset corresponds to the dry season
column15: neap tide. Water and suspended matter fluxes
column17: turbidity and particle size distribution (PSD) were measured for 24h at three cross sections along the estuary. These three stations (ST1
column18: ST2 and ST3) are spaced by 10km. ST1 is located near to the river mouth while ST3 is the further upstream. Each station was sampled for 24 hours with cross-river transects measurements every two hours to cover a full diurnal tidal cycle
column19:
starting at ST1 and finishing at ST3.

Each series of measurements consisted of four different steps. Firstly
column20: cross river velocity profiles were measured will a hull-mounted
column21: downward-looking
column22: 1200 kHz Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler (ADCP RDI Workhorse in bottom tracking mode). Secondly
column23: vertical profiles of conductivity and temperature were measured for each transect
column24: in the deep channel at the middle of the river
column29: now released by JFE Advantech Co.
column32: as Rinko-Profiler) at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. Thirdly and parallel to CTD measurements
column33: depth profiles of floc size distribution and concentration were measured using an in-situ laser scattering and transmissometry instrument with a 90% path reduction module (LISST-100X
column39: in order to study the relationship between turbidity and suspended particulate matter concentration
column40:
water samples (~1 L) near subsurface (~1.5 m depth) were collected every four hours with a Niskin bottle. Turbidity of these samples were measured onboard using a Hach 2100Q Turbidimeter.

Les besoins en eau accrus de la population locale et l'intérêt scientifique de la nappe phréatique de la plaine ont conduit en 2011 à la création du programme Via Sahel Hydrogéologie (VSHy) (https://www.viasahel.fr/activites/hydrogeologie).

La base de données des puits de l'ONG Via Sahel (VS) a été initiée pour suivre le creusement de puits (prospection
column7: avancement
column8: financement…) sous la supervision d'Alain Vallet
column9: consultant de l'ONG Via Sahel à Sangha sur le plateau de Bandiagara au Pays Dogon (Mali)
column10: en coordination avec le responsable puits de Via Sahel Toulouse. Parmi les 233 puits de cette base de données VS
column11: creusés entre 1997 et 2020
column12: essentiellement dans la plaine sédimentaire du Séno-Gondo du Pays Dogon
column13:
certains ont mis du temps à être creusés ou ont été abandonnés (présence importante de roche…).

Cette première base de données (BDpuitsVS) a été développée à la création en 2011 du programme Via Sahel Hydrogéologie (VSHy) avec l'adjonction de mesures hydrogéologiques (relèvement précis des positions de puits
column14: des profondeurs d'eau et du fond du puits
column15:
échantillonnage de roche…) dans les puits VS et dans un maximum d'autres puits de la plaine. Cela a abouti à la création de trois autres bases de données puits avec au total 1550 relevés dans 858 puits qui ont permis de cartographier précisément la piézométrie de la nappe phréatique de la plaine et en particulier de développer une application sur téléphone portable de prévision de profondeur de la nappe phréatique.

Quatre bases de données puits ont donc été progressivement élaborées dans le cadre du programme VSHy :

  • la base de données ""Puits"" la plus complète possible propre à Via Sahel (BDpuitsVS-complete-6dec21.xls) qui rassemble un maximum d'informations sur les 233 puits creusés par VS (emplacement
column19: avancement des travaux
column20:
relevés hydrogéologiques…)
  • la base de données ""Puits supplémentaires au pied de la falaise de Bandiagara"" (BDpuitsSup-pied-falaise-2020.xls)
column21:
soit 139 puits correspondant au début de la nappe phréatique de la plaine dans notre zone d'intérêt
  • la base de données des 53 puits creusés dans la plaine par l'ONG partenaire Fraternité Dogon (BDpuitsFD-VSHy-2021.xls)
  • la base de données ""Puits supplémentaires dans la plaine"" (BDpuitsSup-plaine-2021.xls)
